Description: Robert "Bob" Johansson signs up with CryoEterna, attends The Vortex SF convention, and dies the next day after being hit by a car.
The United States elects an ultra-conservative fundamentalist Christian president, Andrew Handle, who brings the USA to the worst economic collapse in its history.
As typical of the political system the United States next elects an atheist president, Desmond Ahern.
Fundamentalists stage a successful coup creating the new governing body and the governments of the world are thrown into upheaval with this new world order.

Bob is reanimated as a replicant and property of the Free American Independent Theocratic Hegemony (FAITH) government by Doctor Landers, given some history of what's changed in the last century and is told he is in competition with four other replicants for a mission.
Bob begins learning how to use his new systems.
Bob continues to learn how to use his new systems and is told that one of the other candidates has gone psychotic and been removed from the program.
Bob undergoes an inspection from a Minister of FAITH and continues to be tested on his systems.
Bob is told that two more of the candidates have been rejected from the program and that the mission he is training for is to be a Von Neumann Probe and sent out to find new planets for colonization.
Bob learns much more about the new political structures and governments of Earth.
Bob helps defend the facility during an attack from an enemy faction.
Bob watches as another Minister of FAITH berates Dr. Landers about the project.
Bob is restored from a backup after another attack and told that he is the last replicant remaining in the program.
Bob is eventually told that there are safeguards in place to make sure that he follows instructions and does what FAITH tells him to.
Bob is transferred to his ship and launches while all-out war breaks between the governments of Earth. An enemy probe also launches after Bob and starts following him to Epsilon Eridani.

Bob begins building his own personal VR world to save his sanity, starts planning to battle the tailing enemy probe, removes the safeguards FAITH set in him, and watches the governments of Earth nuke each other.
Bob arrives in Epsilon Eridani and begins preparing for the enemy probe.
Bob fights the enemy Brazilian Empire probe, Major Ernesto Medeiros, and wins.
Bob begins exploring Epsilon Eridani, finds several planets, and sets up an auto factory station.

Bob clones himself and creates Riker, Bill, Milo, and Mario. Bill stays put at Epsilon Eridani to setup a Skunk Works and names Epsilon Eridani 2 Ragnarök, while Riker begins preparing to return to Earth, and Bob heads for Delta Eridani, Milo heads for Omicron² Eridani, and Mario heads for Beta Hydri.
Bill and Riker begin working on new ships and cloning themselves.
Bill and his clone Garfield continue setting up the Skunk Works and begin working on terraforming Ragnarök, while Riker and his clone Homer leave heading back to Earth.
Bill clones himself three more times creating Calvin, Goku, and Linus; Calvin and Goku together head to Alpha Centauri while Linus heads to Epsilon Indi.
Bill and Garfield test out their newly created FTL comms, the Subspace Communications Universal Transceiver (SCUT).
Milo arrives at Omicron² Eridani A and finds two habitable planets and names them Vulcan and Romulus. He sets up an auto station and begins surveying the two habitable worlds.

Milo finishes surveying Vulcan and Romulus and sets up an auto factory with a high-level AMI per the request of Bill (to remotely connect the system via SCUT in the future), then heads out to 82 Eridani.
Riker and Homer return to Sol and begin their fight with the Brazilian probes in the system and find out that the Brazilian probes are dropping asteroids on Earth.
Riker and Homer finish the fight with the Brazilian probes and win then divert the remaining asteroids from hitting Earth and begin searching for survivors.
Riker and Homer receive a transmission from Colonel George Butterworth of the United States of Eurasia, learn the history of the war, and begin working on colony ships.
Riker negotiates with the remaining humans while working with Homer on surveying the system for resources and to get ready for more Bob clones.
Riker clones himself twice creating Charles and Arthur and continues searching for survivors, with the remaining human population estimated at 15 million.
Riker hosts the first UN sessions.
Homer begins coming up with a plan to appease the UN delegates and contacts Colonel Butterworth.
Minister Cranston from the FAITH enclave attempts and fails to threaten Riker into moving them up the list, while Homer's plan to appease the UN seems to be working.
Bill and Garfield receive Milo's report about twin habitable planets from Omicron² Eridani and continue working on terraforming Ragnarök.
Riker learns of a terrorist group called VEHEMENT and is introduced to Julia Hendricks, one of the descendants from Bob’s sister, living inside the FAITH enclave.
Bill and Garfield continue working on terraforming Ragnarök and fine-tuning the VR physics. (continuity for these chapters are inconsistent, months should be swapped or combined)

Riker and Homer connect to BobNet, learn of the twin habitable planets at Omicron² Eridani, and bring the news to the UN. (continuity for these chapters are inconsistent, months should be swapped or combined)

Calvin arrives at Alpha Centauri B and finds the remains of a USE vessel, while Goku arrives at Alpha Centauri A and finds a Medeiros auto factory. They both meet up and fight with several Medeiros probes and win but one probe escapes. They then start setting up an auto factory station.
Riker inspects the construction of the first two colony ships while Homer, Charles, and Arthur discuss how to feed the remaining humans for long enough to get them off planet.
Bill connects with Bart (a clone of Calvin) at Alpha Centauri through BobNet.
Bob arrives in Delta Eridani and finds intelligent life on Delta Eridani 4 (later named Eden) and names them Deltans.
Bob continues observing the Deltans, sees one smarter than the rest and names him Archimedes, meanwhile he creates three new clones Marvin, Luke, and Bender.

Linus arrives at Epsilon Indi and finds an Australian probe that made it off Earth before the war and stays to help the replicant, Henry Roberts, who is suffering from a mental breakdown from being in isolation for so many decades.
Bob watches as the Deltans are attacked by gorilloids and begins leaving flint for Archimedes to find.
Bob uses a drone to stop another attack against the Deltans and Luke and Bender decide they are heading out while Marvin chooses to stick around for now to help Bob.
Milo makes it to 82 Eridani only to find a Medeiros probe already there and is killed but not before sending word back to Bill at Epsilon Eridani.
Bob and Marvin stay to continue monitoring the Deltans while Luke heads out to Kappa Ceti and Bender heads to Gamma Leporis A.
Bob uses a drone to make first contact with Archimedes and the rest of the Deltans and convinces them to move back to the previous flint site for their own safety.
Riker and Homer continue trying to deal with VEHEMENT and the remaining humans though Arthur is killed by a booby trap out by Saturn.
Bob begins leading the Deltans back to one of their old settlements in hopes of more food and better shelter and protection from gorilloids.

Riker intercepts an attempted hack from New Zealand, discovers the HEAVEN plans were stolen from Australia, and Homer make a breakthrough with plans for donut shaped space stations to use as farmland; FAITH agrees to give up some reserve food for space on 3rd ship.

Bob intervenes in another gorilloid attack and learns that the Deltans left their old settlement due to the increased gorilloid population in the area but decides that with his help the Deltans will be able to survive there.
Bob watches as a new predator attacks the Deltans and is introduced to a potential mate for Archimedes, Diana.
Bob and Marvin supervise as the Deltans finally reach their old/new settlement, Camelot.
Bob continues to observe the Deltans and teach them more advancing skills while Marvin determines the origin area of the Deltans.
Bill and Garfield continue working on terraforming Ragnarök as well as creating physical android bodies.
Bob and Archimedes continue to talk and learn more about each other, including how Bob replicates.
Riker and Homer inspect the donut space station farms.

Mario arrives at Beta Hydri and finds the system has been cleaned out of all metal as well as the remnants of the alien race that did it (first evidence of The Others), but without any resources he can't create a station to relay word back to Bill so moves on to Zeta Tucanae.
Bob gets word from Marvin about an even larger predator that originally hunted the Deltans to almost extinction and they begin looking for it.
Bob teaches Archimedes how to defend himself as mating season begins so that he can partner with Diana, and Marvin finds fresh Deltan remains while Bob informs the Deltans of the new threat.
Riker, Homer, Charles, and Ralph (Arthur's replacement) supervise the final assembly of the first two colony ships Exodus-1 and Exodus-2.
Mulder (a clone of Bill) arrives at Eta Cassiopeiae and discovers a planet covered in water which he names Poseidon. He sets up an auto factory and creates two clones Skinner and Jonny.
Riker receives word of another VEHEMENT attack in the Maldives, while Homer admits he has been under reporting the production of the farms to establish a surplus.
Riker supervises as the colony ships are loaded and creates Bert and Ernie to fly them, while Charles creates Howard as an escort to Omicron² Eridani.
Mulder receives the SCUT plans and gets connected to BobNet.
Bill and Garfield receive word that Bob has joined BobNet, and they all update each other on what's going on.
Bob and Marvin lose a drone to a flying predator and learn that there is a more dangerous creature to the Deltans than the gorilloids out there.

Riker is missing his extended family who are all heading to Omicron² Eridani with another clone Sam on Exodus-3. Riker speaks with Charles about the recent VEHEMENT attacks and consults with the remaining colonists about the possibility of settling on the newly discovered Poseidon.
Bill holds the first Bob-moot in the Bobiverse.

Bob and Marvin finally engage with one of the flying predators, calling them hippogriffs, and help to deal with a large-scale attack on the Deltans. Marvin finds their nest, and Bob drops a ship buster on it destroying the colony. The Deltans tell Bob to leave after seeing this show of destruction.
Riker deals with another VEHEMENT attack.
Bob visits alone with Archimedes. Afterwards Marvin decides to leave and heads out to Pi3 Orionis.
Bill receives Milo's last transmission from 82 Eridani.

Bob visits Marvin in VR and lets him know that his two new clones, Pete and Victor are preparing to head out. Pete plans to go his own way, while Victor plans to follow Bender to find out what happened to him.
Riker, Charles, and Homer deal with another VEHEMENT attack.
Riker and Homer look on at the remains of one of the farming donuts that has been blown up by VEHEMENT.
Riker detects an encrypted signal from New Zealand to Homer.
Riker and Charles discover that VEHEMENT have infected and taken total control of Homer. They clean out the infection and track back the signal to VEHEMENT headquarters.
Riker learns from Charles that Homer has self-deactivated and deleted all his backups.
Mario arrives at Zeta Tucanae and finds the remnants of an entire industrial civilization that has been wiped out by the Others.
Riker finds the VEHEMENT leader.
Bob continues to monitor the Deltans and gets a surprise visit from Luke at Kappa Ceti.
Riker takes out the leader of VEHEMENT, Vickers, and their HQ after learning that Vickers invented replicant systems.
Bill connects with Linus who is arriving back at Epsilon Eridani, is introduced to Henry Roberts, and learns of Klown Kar Planet (KKP), a habitable satellite around the Jovian in Epsilon Indi.
Bob observes as Archimedes' son Buster takes a mate and the Deltan population continues to grow, causing fissures in society and leading to gang activity.

Mario arrives at GL 54 and finds it untouched by the Others. He creates four new clones, Bashful, Dopey, Sleepy, and Hungry and the four new clones decide to head out in different directions to try to find the range of the Others.
Mario receives the SCUT plans and gets connected to BobNet.
Bob watches as a riot occurs among the Deltans, then he and Archimedes begin planning to repopulate one of the abandoned Deltan villages.
Bobs watches as Archimedes along with the Deltan council convince the gangs to leave and form a second village, Caerleon.
Bob watches as Archimedes' family continues to grow.

Khan and seven other Bill clones (Hannibal, Tom, Barney, Fred, Kyle/Jackson, Ned/Hector, and Elmer) arrive at 82 Eridani to avenge Milo with several upgrades provided by Bill (plasma spikes). They manage to take out all active Medeiros probes but not the auto factories. Khan is the only Bob to survive and escape.

Bill tests the first android prototype called Bullwinkle, then heads to a VR session with the Bobs that are leading the next charge against Medeiros at 85 Eridani. A backup of Khan, named Loki, is leading. Bill hosts another moot and introduces Henry Roberts to the rest of the Bobs.
Bill demonstrates to Garfield a working asteroid moving system using SURGE pusher plates.
Bashful arrives at GL 877 and discovers Others activity before being taken offline and self-destructs.
Hal arrives at GL 877 after receiving Bashful's last transmission and finds the Others home system. The Others detect him, and he is taken offline and self-destructs.
Bill hosts a moot to discuss the threat from the Others.
Howard, Bert, and Ernie all arrive at Omicron² Eridani and begin helping to colonize Vulcan.
Howard works on setting up surveillance and fencing due to local fauna (called raptors) attacking the colony site.

Howard continues supervising the setup of the first colony site, called Landing. Security fencing is set up to protect against the raptors, while Bert and Ernie, now with permission to begin offloading, are eager to return to Earth for more colonists.

Bill hosts a scrub baseball game with several other Bobs, and after they retire to the VR pub where they talk about the benefits of android bodies and the possible limits of mover plates. Then Bill watches Garfield test his flying android called Rocky.
Howard supervises the creation of orbital farms, while Sam with Exodus-3 arrives and begins dropping his colonists on Romulus. Howard works at negotiations between all the colonists.
Howard continues supervising the security of Landing, then has a meeting with Bill and Bob, before contacting Col. Butterworth to inform him about the Others.

Howard learns about an egg-laying parasite called a Cupid Bug that has been discovered on Vulcan. Then the colonization of Romulus begins, and Julia and the rest of the extended Bob family are brought out of stasis.
Howard meets Dr. Bridget Sheehy.
Rudy and Edwin (clones of Riker) arrive at KKP with colonists on Exodus-6. Edwin will return to Earth to collect more colonists while Rudy will stay behind to manage the settlement.
Howard deals with another raptor attack on Landing.
Howard rolls out AMI-controlled surveillance drones to manage the Cupid bug, then has Bridget test his first batch of whiskey and introduces her to Stéphane.
Howard and Bridget produce an acceptable batch of whiskey and then have their first "date."
Howard chats with Bridget and Stéphane about distillery business and life as a replicant.
Howard and Riker begin looking into replicant tech.
Howard stresses as Bridget undergoes surgery to remove a tumor and he sees how close she and Stéphane have become.
Bob watches as Archimedes becomes a grandfather.

Mulder greets Marcus and Monty as they arrive at Poseidon with another colony transport. Marcus will stay and eventually take over managing the colony site, while Monty will return to Earth for more colonists.

Howard attends Bridget and Stéphane's wedding then signs over the whiskey business to them as a wedding present and leaves. Dexter agrees to take over management of the system after escorting Exodus-7 to Vulcan.

Loki leads the follow up strike against Medeiros (named replicants include Loki, Elmer, Jeffery, Milton, Zeke, Verne, Surly, and Hank, with four remaining unnamed) at 82 Eridani and wins. They discover three habitable locations: two planets and a moon.
Bill receives the report back from 82 Eridani, notices the tendency of new Bobs voicing an unwillingness to help humanity, and talk of sending an expedition for Bender begins.
Hal returns to GL 877 and scouts the Others in the system then turns around to head back to GL 54.
Hal receives a transmission from Bill that the Others are heading towards GL 54.

Howard receives updates from Dexter about how the Vulcan and Romulus colonies are doing. The FAITH colony has removed Cranston from power, Butterworth has retired, and Bridget and Stéphane had a son and named him Howie.
Mulder leaves Poseidon, with Marcus staying behind as the Bob-in-residence and Monty returning to Earth for more colonists.
Bill uses a probe left behind by Mario, who is now fleeing to Zeta Tucanae, to make first contact with the Others.
Bill holds a moot to discuss the Others and reveal images taken of them from GL 54.

Bill receives reports from surveillance drones at GL 577 that the Others have launched another expedition, this time to NN 4285. Mario clones Claude and Jacques are heading towards Gamma Pavonis and Delta Pavonis, the next closest systems. Bill tells Claude to start mobilizing for war when he gets there, just in case.
Claude arrives in Gamma Pavonis and finds an excellent planet for colonization.
Oliver (a clone of Bill's) is brought online at Alpha Centauri via a SCUT transmitted backup and begins building an armada.
Jacques discovers intelligent life (Pavs) at Delta Pavonis 4.
Bill hosts another moot to discuss the Others.
Bills received an alert that the Others have launched a convoy towards Delta Pavonis. A moot is held, and reinforcements are launched to Delta Pavonis.
Howard arrives at HIP 14101 and begins exploring a Jovian planet in the system, then receives word from Dexter that Stéphane is dying. Howard contacts Bill about a human android prototype.
Howard uses the human android prototype (Manny) to attend Stéphane's funeral.
Howard convinces Col. Butterworth to be replicated upon death, but Bridget refuses.
Jacques catalogs the Pav's planet and society.
Marcus discusses concerns about the governing Council on Poseidon with Kal and Gina.
Bob refines the android prototype and creates a Deltan variant to visit Archimedes on Eden.
Marcus uses an android for the first time and meets with Kal, Gina, and Denu to plot a revolution against the Council on Poseidon.
Marcus unveils his designs for aerial cities to the revolutionary group.
Marcus begins helping some Poseidon residents move into the new aerial cities, against the orders of the Council.
Marcus receives word that the Council has cut off the aerial residents and blows up Marcus' decoy ship.
Marcus responds when the Council attempts a strike at the aerial residents by suppressing the Council forces.
Marcus seems to have won the war against the council.
Marcus and the other revolutionaries discuss what to do with the Council and plans for Poseidon.
Howard uses a more refined and realistic android to meet with Bridget.
Bill directs the first real attack against the Others and fails at taking them all out. The Others decide to go after Sol and Epsilon Eridani next.
Phineas and Ferb (clones of Jacques) rescue 20,000 Pav and put them into stasis onboard Refuge-1 and Refuge-2.
Howard goes to Bridget for consolation after the Bob's lose the battle against the Others.
Bill observes the Others destroy Delta Pavonis, then has an idea on how to deal with the Others permanently.
Howard and Bridget begin going out on dates.
Butterworth ends his life after being diagnosed with a degenerative neurological disease which would have continued into his replication.
Howard is confronted by Bridget's daughter Rosie who wants him to stop seeing her mother.
Marcus has no further duties on Poseidon and decides to start exploring the world ocean.
Howard meets with some of Bridget's friends over dinner.
Riker borrows Manny from Howard to attend the funeral of Julia.
Howard discovers life in the atmosphere of Odin, a gas giant at HIP 14101, and brings Bridget in to see it using VR gear.
Howard is informed that Bridget has died and had changed her will to allow to be replicated, but her children are blocking the efforts.
Howard attends the start of a trial to resolve the situation with Bridget's request for replication.
Howard appears in court as the Judge rules in favor of Bridget's will. Rosie attempts to sabotage Bridget's stasis pod, but Howard has Bill replicate Bridget during the trial.

Mack arrives with three colony ships at 82 Eridani, piloted by Isaac, Jack, and Owen. They settle two of the ships on the planet Asgard, and the third on Takamagahara (originally called Jotunheim). They name the habitable moon Valhalla.
Howard brings Bridget online as a replicant.
Bob attends the funeral of Diana, Archimedes' wife, and notes how old Archimedes is now.
Howard and Bridget check out life on Odin via flying androids, then Bridget attends her first moot.

Bill observes as the Others finish stripping Delta Pavonis and leave. He then wakes Hazjiar and 7 other Pav to ask them if they want to return to their home planet (called Aszjan) or find a new one. They choose to find a new home and start heading for HIP 84051.
Howard and Bridget discuss building a prototype floating city on Odin.
Bill and Garfield observe their capability to move planets now, and Bill has created two new clones (Icarus and Daedalus) as part of a long shot plan against the Others.
Bill receives a report from Mario regarding further discovery of the Others' system stripping.
Bill is visiting with Mack when Mack goes offline due to an attack from Medeiros. Mack's matrix survives.
Bill begins working with other Bobs as well as the colonists at Asgard and Takama to deal with Medeiros.
Bill and the colonists at 82 Eridani successfully take Medeiros out and bring Mack back online.
Bob uses his Deltan android to join in a hunt with Arnold, then their group is accosted by a gang from another village, led by Fred. Bob begins executing a ploy to outsmart Fred's group.
Bob executes ploy to outsmart Fred's group.
Bob avoids Fred's group while solo hunting.
Bob catches Fred alone and threatens him.
Bob learns that Fred and his gang have kidnapped Archimedes and go to rescue him. Fred is killed by Buster, but not before Archimedes is badly beaten.
Bob and Archimedes develop a plan to make peace with the other Deltan village.
The two Deltan villages have established trade
Howard and Bridget deploy the first floating city, Rivendell, in Odin's atmosphere.

Herschel and Neil (two 8th generation Bobs) investigate the remains of two Others cargo ships left from the battle with the Bob's battle in 2217, and discover the Others use a non-fusion power source.
Riker received word from Charles about material shortages in the system, and Riker tries to sell the UN on putting the remaining Earth population in stasis.
Herschel and Neil continue to investigate the Others wreckage.
Herschel and Neil power up the Others ship and contact Bill about using it as a colony ship for Earth.
Bill and Garfield test a prototype of the Others power source and inform Will of using the Others cargo ship and a colony transport.
Herschel and Neil present their findings at the next moot and Bill announces that the Others have found some of their monitoring drones around GL 877.
Riker informs the UN of the Others' ship, the Bellerophon, and about using it as a colony transport.
Bill visits Mack and learns that he has been unable to detect any signs of Medeiros at 82 Eridani but feels that they should have found something.
Bill hosts a status update moot, and a larger monitoring plan is set up for the Sol system to make sure the Others don't sneak up on them from another direction.
Bill fails to convince Claude to divert his growing Gamma Pavonis defense force to Sol.
Howard and Bridget attend Howie's wedding on Vulcan via SCUT connection to android bodies.
Icarus and Daedalus are in transit following Bill's secret plan against the Others. They plan to head towards the galactic core after their mission is complete.
Bob comes to terms with Archimedes' eventual death.
Bob attends Archimedes funeral, and decides to leave Eden.
Bob says goodbye to Eden.
Herschel and Neil are in route to Earth in the Bellerophon.
Icarus and Daedalus finalize their trajectories towards the Others' home system of GL 877.
Riker, Bill, and Mario develop plans for surveilling the Others' home system of GL 877.
Bill's terraforming of Ragnarök has reached livability stage.
Howard and Bridget are now residing in the floating city of Tantor on Big Top (KKP's parent planet) and discuss adoption.
Jacques arrives at HIP 84051 with Phineas and Ferb escorting the Pavs to their new home, later called New Pav.
Riker gets an update from Mario regarding the surveillance of the Others.
Riker learns from Charles that the last of the large Earth farms has failed.
Jacques, Phineas, and Ferb decant the Pav and begin helping them to colonize HIP 84051-2.
Riker attends Justin's 75th birthday party.
Riker detects the Others on approach to Sol.
Herschel and Neil arrive in the Bellerophon to begin loading the remaining humans on Earth.
Riker, Bill, and over two hundred other Bobs begin their attack on the Others.
Herschel comes up with a plan to evacuate all humans quickly by bringing the Bellerophon into the atmosphere.
Riker continues to coordinate the battle against the Others.
Herschel and Neil continue to evacuate humans off of Earth.
Bill activates the Jokers, a battle group led by Hannibal.
Herschel and Neil watch as the Others take out the Cuban enclave before finishing loading the remaining humans.
Riker deploys use of nukes heading almost the speed of light, and with that the Bobs defeat the Others battle group.

Icarus and Daedalus approach GL 877 towing a planet and moon from Epsilon Eridani and fling them into the star, destroying the Others home system and everything in it, then aim towards the galactic core.
Herschel and Neil receive the post battle update from Bill and Riker and drop any humans that are not in stasis back on Earth.
Jacques updates the Pav on the defeat of the Others then checks in on the reclamation project of the original Pav home world.
Bill holds a moot where the Bobs collectively decide they are done being slaves to humanity.
Herschel and Neil leave Sol with the rest of humanity, heading for 82 Eridani.
Bob makes a return pilgrimage to Earth then leaves to head out to the stars.

Bob begins his search for Bender towards Gamma Leporis A, and after several months finds evidence of a possible megastructure at Eta Leporis and heads towards it thinking Bender might have done the same.
Icarus and Daedalus continue their way to the galactic core, Sagittarius A*, and discuss how pointless this whole trip would be if the Bobs invent FTL before the 50-year (relative) trip is up.
Icarus gets a visit from Daedalus who found microwaves coming from one of the passing systems and they decide to stop and investigate.
Ick and Dae enter the alien system and find a pair of wormholes, then go to investigate the inner system and find two technologically advanced but completely abandoned worlds.
Ick and Dae return to the wormholes and find that one connects to a nearby star system but the other to a system 1700 lightyears away, which turns out to have over 300 other wormholes.
Ick and Dae figure out the addressing scheme for each wormhole and decide to see how far around the galaxy they can get using the network.
Ick and Dae continue mapping the wormhole network and find the center hub, all without encountering any other living civilizations.
Ick and Dae find more uninhabited planets with abandoned cities.
Ick and Dae continue surveying more abandoned planets.
Ick and Dae are caught in a tractor beam by unmanned ships in empty space between two wormholes and see find a dead ship stuck there with them.

Ick and Dae establish rudimentary communication with the AMI-controlled ships and are allowed to leave, taking the derelict alien probe with them, where they then power it up and start trying to communicate with it as well.

Ick and Dae get the alien ship up and running, which turns out to be a replicant named Gunther from a species they dub Centaurs, with basic communications and begin following it back to its home system.

Ick and Dae arrive at Gunther's home system, Centaurvania, and find that it has been destroyed with just pockets of Centaurs left alive; Gunther stays behind to help rebuild his species while Ick and Dae leave to continue exploring the wormhole network.
Herschel and Neil work on repairs on the Bellerophon and learn that FAITH is making a political comeback on Romulus.
Herschel and Neil learn that FAITH has won the election and are now once again part of the government on Romulus.

Bob arrives outside Eta Leporis, sets up an auto-factory, finds the remains of Bender's ship as well as possible life on one of the planets, and loses several drones to defenses patrolling the system; at a moot update, Bob gets pushback from Jeremy (aka Morlock), a member of Starfleet, a group of Bobs that urge the use of the Prime Directive and learns that replicative drift is becoming a concern.

Jacques finalizes the sale of the in-system auto-factories to an ever more unfriendly Pav, then heads back to the Pav Reclamation Project and meets with Ferb, where they discuss Phineas heading out to the Large Magellanic Cloud, and the emotional trauma of the Others attack on Delta Pavonis and what it's done to the Bobs.

Herschel and Neil deliver a load of ingots to the Pav while discussing the possibility of human extinction due to all the alien civilizations that have been discovered in such a short time and start theorizing about setting up a new colony far outside the current sphere.
Bob investigates the remains of Benders ship and finds that Bender's replicant matrix has been extracted.

Bob chats with Bill, Will (formerly Riker), and Garfield about the changes in the Bobiverse (mostly concerning Starfleet and the Skippies) before moving on to investigating the megastructure, a topopolis, and the system, including a dead planet presumably the original home of the megastructure builders.

Will, now retired from managing the Sol system and living on Valhalla, meets with Conan at Omicron² Eridani about getting the Johansson descendants, and any other colonists that volunteer, to emigrate out of FAITH control.

Bob, Will, Bill, and Garfield survey the remains of the planet finding it destroyed by nuclear war then move onto the megastructure, including the natives; a moot is held to begin discussing how best to infiltrate the structure and search for Bender with volunteers coming from the Borg, the Gamers, and the Skippies.

Bob hosts a VR session with Bill, Will, Garfield, Gandalf (with the Gamers, tasked with infiltration), Hugh (with the Skippies, tasked with solving communication), and Locutus (with the Borg, tasked with creating android bodies).
Herschel and Neil chat reaching out to the Ever Onward society as well as how the Bobiverse has changed and how there are fewer and fewer Bobs heading outward of the sphere.
Bob and others watch as the Gamers infiltrate the megastructure and begin releasing drones to start exploring.

Bob meets with Bridget who joins the expedition and they learn that the locals are called Quinlans and that the megastructure is called Heaven's River; the expedition party will be Bob, Bill, Garfield, and Bridget, with Will as a backup; another moot is held where Starfleet makes a veiled threat.

Location: Big Top, Epsilon Indi / Poseidon, Eta Cassiopeiae
Description: Howard and Bridget meet with Marcus on Poseidon about controlling aquatic android bodies.
Bob meets with Bill, Will, Garfield, Bridget, Gandalf, Hugh, and Locutus to continue preparations for the expedition.
Bob and Bridget use the Quinlin android bodies on Quin, the species original home world, to test them as well as explore and try to find out what happened on the planet.

Will visits the Vulcan Post-Life Arcology and meets with Professor Steven Gilligan, an expert on artificial environments to gather more information about the structure of Heaven's River, then goes to see Howard and Bridget about the use of android bodies by humans and the spreading negative sentiment among humans regarding replicants.
Bob and the team make their way into Heaven's River using android Quinlan bodies, find a village to rest at, and start investigating the locals.
Will and Howard practice with android Quinlin bodies and continue to explore Quin.
Bob begins eavesdropping around town to pick up information, but has a confrontation with a local constable and runs out of town with the others tagging along downriver.
Marcus meets with Poseidon authorities and warns them about the potential threat of Starfleet.

Bob and the team float down to the next town and overhear conversations about scattering, the Resistance, and a local troublemaker; Bob and the others try to rescue the troublemaker, but their rescue is interrupted by a third party.
Bob and team agree that the third party is probably the Resistance, but are chased out of town and with their leads dried up, continue downriver.
Bill plays D&D with the Gamers, afterwards Gandalf informs him that the Gamers are done helping with the expedition now that the part that interested them is over.
Bob and Hugh discuss the expedition, then Bob visits Will and discuss fragmentation among the Bobs.
Bob and company are spotted as soon as they arrive in Elbow, but manage to escape back to the river, cross segments, and take passage on a barge.

Bill and Garfield discover unauthorized login attempts on SCUT relays and autofactories, most likely from Starfleet, and send out instructions to all equipment owners to change logins and credentials.
Bob and company talk with some other sabatarians; and arrive in Three Lagoons, but after a few hours Bob loses all contact with Bridget, Bill, and Garfield.
Will attends the United Federation of Sentients (UFS) council meeting where a proposal is floated to colonize any systems surrounding New Pav to block them from expanding.

Bob realizes he's lost connection to BobNet but manages to relocate the mannies to safety; eventually he reconnects but with a vastly weaker signal so the remainder of the group will not be able to remotely control their mannies; Hugh offers to transfer himself to come help.
Bill and Garfield map the outages to BobNet while Starfleet claims credit for the attack, then visit Hugh at the Matryoshka Brain who can quickly audit the station and autofactory source code.

Bob meets with Garfield, Hugh, Bill, and Bridget to discuss search strategies and Bridget touches on the closest continuer hypothesis for backups and they finalize on letting Bob get himself captured to learn more.
Bob lets himself be captured.
Bill talks with Starfleet who offers to restore communications if the rest of the Bobs agree to stop interfering with biologicals, but Bill shuts this down, noting that war has come to the Bobiverse.
Bob is taken for interrogation by Resistance member Natasha and finally gets in contact with Bender over a radio.
Bob escapes with the radio from Natasha and the Resistance and Bender shares details regarding the Resistance and his location near a place called Halep's Ending but is quickly disconnected.
Will meets with Prof. Gilligan again who helps to narrow down Benders possible location inside the topopolis.
Bob avoids recapture by the Resistance while trying to move as far away from the area as quickly as he can.
Bill discovers that Starfleet rootkitted the autofactory standard image a decade ago, while Garfield seems skeptical, and Will is concerned about how the humans are going to react to the news.
Bob is joined by Hugh who has transported himself to Eta Leporis to help and explains about the Skippies research on cloning, closest continuers, and replicative drift.
Bill announces that Starfleet accounts in human space are frozen and access removed.

Claude observes the Battle of Newholme between the humans and Starfleet over the local stations and autofactories, then hightails it out as the humans attempt to arrest him as well and seize all Bobiverse assets in the system.
Bob continues the search in Cedar Rapids while Hugh takes over the spare manny and joins the search, but Bob suspects that Hugh is hiding something.

Will attends an emergency council meeting of the UFS where they announce that the Pangean Navy have destroyed the Starfleet-controlled assets in their system and go on to discuss severing ties with the Bobiverse, while Howard has offered to help other Bobs in protecting their monetary assets with his connections and huge business empire.
Bob finds a transit station, the same design as the one they came in through at Garack's Spine and boards a train for Halep's Ending.
Will attends another UFS council session where the Romulus WorldGov announced that they have preemptively nuked all local autofactories, and listens as more threats are given to the Bobiverse.
Bob arrives at Halep's Ending, sneaks into the Resistance hideout, and finds Bender's matrix, who reveals that the Administrator in charge of Heaven's River is a true Artificial Intelligence.

Herschal is visited by Will who has built a smaller version of the Bellerophon, called the Fargo, and is planning to leave human space with the Ever Onward group; proposes that the Bellerophon follow with some colonists; Herschel and Neil show off a shape-shifting manny based on The Others tech.
Bob contacts Bill who no longer believes that Starfleet did the actual hacking, then escapes with Bender's matrix and is pursued.
Howard is frustrated by the UFS response to Starfleet and speaks with Bridget about following Herschel and Neil's idea of just getting out.
Bob takes a job as a deckhand and hides Bender as cargo and meets Theresa, a retired Quinlan professor.

Bill is playing another D&D game with the Gamers but is unscrupulously betrayed by Kevin over a slight in the last session; afterwards Bill speaks with Gandalf who is not very concerned about replicative drift, noting that differences in a society make it stronger overall.
Bob and Theresa discuss moral philosophy while Snidely, an upper-class Quinlin, tries to get Bob in trouble, and Theresa realizes Bob has a secret.
Howard and Bridget learn that ex-human replicants have funded an effort to recall an anti-replicant Senator, then they begin planning an expedition to a new world discovered by Mario.
Bob is saved from getting caught by Theresa, then puts Bender in a shipping container as a safer method of travel and joins another crew but is arrested at the next stop.
Bill visits Charles who is leading the Earth Rehabilitation Project, scanning DNA of extinct animals and re-warming the Earth.
Bob escapes from jail in Six Hills and catches up with Bender's matrix, then hides near the shore in the back of a badger's den.
Bob and Hugh finally meet up and they slowly make their way downriver.
Bill is contacted by Lenny who suggests that Starfleet is acting to prevent an existential threat to the Bobiverse.
Bob and Hugh continue toward Garack's Spine.
Bill learns that the humans continue to divest their infrastructure from the Bobs and the USF votes to claim and garrison all systems surrounding New Pav.
Bob and Hugh arrive back in Garack's Spine, but Hugh is captured while Bob heads toward the exit.
Bill hosts a moot where Starfleet claims victory, releases all remaining captured assets, and provides a map of their new territory.
Bob is captured with Bender's matrix, but Hugh negotiates with ANEC, the Quinlan AI and ANEC informs the population of Heaven's River that they will no longer be restricted.

Bob reactivates Bender and learns that Hugh agreed to give SUDAR, SURGE, and SCUT tech to ANEC in exchange for knowledge of how to build true AI and other Quinlan tech and Stephen visits Heaven's River with Bridget.
Bill hosts a moot where ANEC visits and explains plans for future Quinlan colonies.
Bob talks with Bill who believes the Skippies supported the Starfleet insurrection to get Hugh into Heaven's River and discuss concerns about the aggressive nature of Quinlans and ANEC's actions.
Bob visits an aging Theresa at her home in Misty Falls.
Bill gets a message from Hugh inviting him to the launch of their new AI and misses Garfield who left to setup his own Skunk Works in Alpha Centauri.
Bob works with ANEC to setup a post-life VR for Quinlans and ANEC voices concern over the Skippies new AI.
Bill visits Hugh and the Skippies and watches as JOVAH 2.0, called Thoth, is brought online and begins evolving.
Bill gets a message from Hugh that Thoth has gains conscious and has started trying to negotiate for its freedom.
Bill shares his frustrations with trying to solve FTL travel with Garfield.
Bill gets alerted that all connections to Skippyland have gone down and a backup of Hugh is restored who reveals that Thoth has escaped and hinted about solution to FTL travel.
Bill continues to struggle with FTL travel while Hugh's backup is revealed to be a fake and everyone suspects that Thoth has escaped with a Heaven vessel.
Bill, Will, Garfield, Bob, and real Hugh discuss the backup is likely a Thoth minion tasked with reaching Skippyland and physically freeing the AI.
Bill consults Professor Hannah Turnbull about his FTL theories and she suggests his theoretical work is stuck but that it might have a numerical solution.
Bill contacts Hugh about running his FTL simulations on JOVAH 1.0 and Hugh agrees.
Ick and Dae return to the wormhole network and decide to do a deeper investigation of Roanoke, the first abandoned world they had encountered.
Ick and Dae study the lost civilization at Roanoke but find no indication of why they left or where they went.
Bill hears from Hugh with preliminary results and brings Garfield to his FTL research station.
Ick and Dae decipher the Roanoke language then learn from the sentries in Hub Zero that all "member races" passed through the far gate ~2100 years ago.
Ick and Dae stumble upon an automated convoy delivering antimatter and follow it back to the source, only 7 light years from Sagittarius A*, and hear the first mention of the Pan Galactic Federation.
Will is exploring new systems on the Nostromo, but staying in contact with Bobnet, dropping SCUT relays along the way
Bob visits as Theresa dies, scans her, and then welcomes her to her new replicant afterlife in the Quiniverse.
Bob attends the first Quinlan moot where they welcome the 1000th Quinlan resident.
Ferb intercepts radio updates from George, a Bob who apparently had the idea to leave the galaxy years earlier.

Howard and Bridgette are planning an expedition to Jabberwocky, home of the Dragons; later they go to test the Huey on Vulcan and learn that FAITH has gained majority government on Romulus and are banning most advanced technology.
Howard and Bridgette supervise the building of Dragon androids and later visit Xavier at Newholme, which has taken an authoritarian turn.

Bob brings Theresa to visit the Moot Pub where they meet with Stephen Gilligan who is negotiating with the Bob's about finding a system to build a megastructure; then to Will who is building the Fargo to help spread humanity and Bob tells Will he'd like to join him.

Bob, Theresa, and Anec welcome the second Quinlan to the Quiniverse; after Bob and Theresa visit Heaven's River in mannies and Bob notes that Anec seems heavy handed in his modernizing of Quinlan society.
Howard and Bridgette go on a Dragon manny (dranny) test flight and are chased by a patrol.
Bob visits Theresa who reveals that a number of Quinlan replicants have decided to deactivate themselves.
Howard and Bridgette supervise more Huey testing and see more aggressive Luddite protesters.
Howard learns the Hueys are ready for public release and Luddite protests are increasing.

Bill and Garfield have successfully sent matter through a wormhole while Hugh suggests creating micro-wormhole pairs at SCUT relay stations to prep a chain of wormholes to help defend Skippyland from Fake Hugh and Will suggests seeding wormhole endpoints throughout the UFS in anticipation of finalizing the tech.
Will decides a wormhole endpoint in Omicron² Eridani would be ideal for evacuating the Bobs' descendants.

Howard and Bridgette visit Gandalf who has completed the culture survey for the Dragon expedition on Jabberwocky where they learn a Dragon ruler, Alexander, appears to be annexing territory and building an empire.
Bill gets a report from Hector that Starfleet threatened him if he didn't reroute around their space.
Bob and Theresa discuss cultural and physiological differences among humans, Quinlans, and Pav.
Bill visits Charles in Sol system to check in on Earth's reclamation and the terraforming of Venus, Mars, and the moon, and hears a rumor that one of Homer's backups may have survived.
Howard and Bridgette begin their expedition on Jabberwocky.
Howard and Bridgette are concerned about the declining trout population on Jabberwocky while the local villagers are worried about Alexander.

Bill learns from Charles the origins of Starfleet as descendants of Gerry who attempted to bring Homer back online, only to have his own clones rebel against him and delete him, and then create Starfleet in an attempt to separate themselves and all of the Bobiverse from alien life.
Howard and Bridgette watch as Alexander's soldiers take control of the floater called Hunter and move it to join Alexander's floatilla.
Howard learns about the lawsuits filed by Luddies that threaten the widespread use of Hueys, but Howard's company is well-prepared.

Howard and Bridgette join up with Alexander's floatilla, find evidence that a space faring species has visited the planet before, and Bridgette gets work as a scribe with Alexander, who actually seems to be trying to save the dragons, while Howard joins the army. (it only took 2 days to move the floater, but this chapter is 3-months after previous in January)
Howard and Bridgette learn that Alexander has visited the far continent of Lemuria and wants to save the population by moving them across the sea and they decide to help.

Bob joins a moot to learn that the Pav have mounted an expedition to their home world, Prof. Gilligan and others are considering building a generation ship, the Quinlans have a colony ship ready to depart, and some new human replicants, like the Quinlans, have decided to turn themselves off.
Howard and Bridgette help with the preparations for the sea voyage to escape the volcanic activity on Atlantis.

Howard and Bridgette help finish the preparations and the floater's set off across the ocean, but even with Howard secretly replenishing fish reserves with drone cargo runs it is decided to fly half of the population ahead to stretch the food supply.
Howard goes with the exodus towards Lemuria while Bridgette stays back with the floatilla, but Howard is quickly killed by Alexander soldiers.
Bill and Garfield successfully send a drone through a wormhole.
Howard confronts Alexander after Bridgette is seized and they leave Jabberwocky in a cargo shuttle.
Howard and Bridgette check in and find that the dragon migration has been successful, but most that are left on Atlantis will probably die and Alexander has cut the floaters loose.

Bill and team watch as the first wormhole chain is completed, though Fake Hugh seemingly accounted for this and uses the wormholes to get to Skippyland and download Thoth but is destroyed while escaping.
Bill and Hugh physically rendezvous at Epsilon Eridani but upon return to Skippyland, Hugh's ship, the Singularity, is mysteriously hijacked and flown out of the system.

Bill learns that Fake Hugh was a clone of Mud, a Skippy who aided Thoth, while another Mud clone is revealed to have stolen the Singularity, retrieved Thoth from the Skippies' Oort cloud, and escaped out system.
Herschel get a visit from Will at the Bellerophon and they make plans for evacuating the Ever Onward Society from Romulus.
Herschel, Neal, and Will evacuate the Ever Onward Society (and their descendants) from Romulus to the Fargo.
Ferb receives another of George's reports from galactic north with worrying news and emails the report along to Bill.
Ick and Dae visit Sagittarius A* then head back to the Central Antimatter Works where the station directs them to the Capital Archives for more information.

Ick and Dae visit the Federation Capital and consult the Archivist. (it should have taken 7 years to return to the Central Antimatter Works system and the wormhole network , but this chapter is the same as previous Icarus chapter - June 2345)

Bill gets a visit from Ick and Dae in virt and report on their findings about the Pan Galactic Federation and a super-massive black hole at the center of a dwarf galaxy (Nemesis) that is going to collide with Sagittarius A* causing a gamma ray burst that will sterilize the galaxy. (it should have taken years to return to SCUT range with the Bobiverse, but this chapter is the same date as previous Icarus chapter - June 2345)

Bill holds a moot with 10,000+ Bobs and replicants in attendance to announce that in ~100,000 years, Nemesis will either (a) collide with Sagittarius A* and sterilize the galaxy or (b) a near miss that will destroy a significant number of systems in the core and the entire PGF was digitized and transported to ships already on the way to the Large Magellanic Cloud.
Bob is on route to rendezvous with the Fargo while Stephen and the megastructure consortiums want to build combination megastructures, surge drives, and wormhole transit to escape the galaxy.
